name: Palette Hi Hotel Bangkok
detail: The hotel environment was nice, clean, and comfortable.

name: Solaria Nishitetsu Hotel Bangkok
detail: Overall, the service standards here are truly outstanding.

name: The Berkeley Hotel Pratunam
detail: This is my second visit since last year. Showed some improvement at receptionist counter (More attentive and polite). Check in and out was fast, paid deposit of THB2000 for 3 nights by credit card which will be refund when you check out (Hotel in Bangkok need deposit during stay, so is ok for me). Booked Luxury Room at Main Tower, rooms are spacious, clean with stable wifi and clean big bath room with clean bathtub, everything as show in Agoda website. Only downside is the bathroom, very slippery floor, so be careful everyone. (I nearly fell a few times during my stay, hope something can be done before accident happens for other guest). Last of all, this hotel is very close to most shopping center in Pratunam and there are a lot of small night stall outside hotel and famous chicken rice are just opposite of hotel but will need to walk around 15-20 mins to reach nearest BTS. Will still choose to stay here if there's room promotion on my next trip to Bangkok.

name: Grande Centre Point Surawong Bangkok
detail: One of the standout features of the hotel is its facilities.

name: Diamond Bangkok Apartment
detail: I liked the location. Usually I have stayed in the Banglamphu area, the backpacker place to stay. After staying there for the last 25 years whenever in Bangkok I felt like I needed a change so chose Siam area. I arrived very early, 7am, I was able to take a room at 9am. The staff at reception were not the friendliest, but at least I got my room early. The road in front of the hotel is very busy, but the room was quiet enough. The usual A/C. fridge, T.V. you expect at this price range. Wifi was good. I don't think the hotel had food, but around I found the best street food and cheap but great food restaurants I have found in Bangkok. The hotel is a ten minute walk to the massive MBK shopping mall and the impressive Siam Discovery Centre. I spent hours walking around these places until my feet were aching too much, how wonderful to have a foot massage later! Anyway, back to the hotel, it's a bit on the expensive side for me but is good value for money. For short stays in Bangkok with plenty of local shopping options I can recommend and will likely stay there again. Also close to the BTS Skytrain and Jim Thompson house.

name: Grand Howard Hotel
detail: My son (15), my two daughters (12 and 9) and me stayed at the Grand Howard Hotel for nine nights. It was our first stay in Asia (even our first stay outside of Europe). The staff of the Grand Howard Hotel was very welcoming. The hotelroom was big, clean and had all comfort you need. We had a corner room in the 17th floor and could enjoy a spectacular view of Bangkok because of the very big windows. My children especially enjoyed the big swimming pool in the 6th floor. Part of it is roofed (protected from the sun) so you can use it at all day times. But the most we loved the staff of the hotel. They gave us good advice about what to see in Bangkok and they gave us a warm welcome every time when we returned to the hotel. We felt like being home. Thanks a lot to the staff of the Grand Howard Hotel!

name: Akara Hotel Bangkok
detail: We stayed in this hotel for 4 nights while in Bangkok. The rooms are absolutely BEAUTIFUL, marble everything, great shower, nice tub for baths, free bath bombs, great feeling sheets. You definitely feel like your staying in luxury, but for such a reasonable price! The staff is very attentive and will rush to open the door for you when they see you coming back, carry all your suitcases for you, etc. For the price I don’t think you will be able to find another hotel in Bangkok that offers this much. The breakfast buffet was amazing, they had a western side and Asian side. In the afternoons they serve snacks available to the guests for free from 2pm-5pm which was nice. I would absolutely stay in this hotel again if I come back to Bangkok!

name: Romance Hotel Sukhumvit 97
detail: Hotel overall was great. Staff was very pleasant. Only thing is the location I would consider very far from central bangkok. Takes roughly 20mins++ bts if you intend.to go from hotel to BTS Siam and longer if you intend to go to Jatujak. Going by Taxi would be costly. The hotel would be ideal if you intend to go to Mega Bangna as you can get there by going to BTS Udom Suk and then taking the free shuttle bus from there. Another thing is walking from BTS Bangchak to the hotel is quite a distance so I wouldnt recommend anyone with a family or have problems walking due to the inconvenience. That aside we had a pleasant stay at the hotel. Would be great if the hotel could provide a buggy ride (i saw a buggy outside which was never used maybe for decor only) or some other form of transport to pick customers up from BTS bangchak to the hotel and back.

name: Boss Suites Nana
detail: I haven't stayed in several hotels in Bangkok anyway, but these were the main features that I was looking for in a hotel. 1) Clean and fresh looking 2) Free fast wifi 3) Good breakfast 4) Friendly staff 5) Good location where it's happening for a Solo traveler like me. Boss suites has it all. It is just the best and is not too pricey. I wouldn't call it a 4 star but it is very clean and well maintained. Since it is a new hotel, the interior, decor etc are really attractive and well planned ( like the see through bathroom just in case u have one of those special guests :). It is still a good hotel for couples and families but not the location) The price through agoda was $41 as advertised but it will be $49 before checkout. That's Agoda not Boss suites. Most of the staff can speak fairly good English and extremely friendly. My experiences with the friendly staff are too many in the two days that I've stayed. There is not one improvement that I can suggest for this hotel and that is not an exaggeration. This definitely is value for money. You can rely on the staff for numerous things like free Tuk-Tuk (They got one for me even after hours), help with getting a cab, help with your room and facilities and also suggestions on going around in Bangkok at the reception. And ya, for all those fun loving, crazy young travelers like me , the hotel offers VIP massages for about 500 Baht. They are all professional masseurs but you can take the massage a litte further if you like to :)

name: Mandarin Hotel Managed by Centre Point
detail: The Mandarin Hotel managed by Centre Point is worthy of its four star rating, unlike a lot of other hotels in SE Asia. The price was very affordable and much cheaper than other hotels in its class with comparable locations. Only one major problem with the hotel: on check-in day, the reception gave me keys to a room already booked and the other people's belongings were still there when I arrived. The limited English of the bellhop meant that I had to go all the way back to the front desk to get things (quickly) fixed. Lastly, and this is probably true for all hotels in Bangkok, DO NOT use the taxi/tok-tok service that hangs out right outside the hotel. Their prices are extortion rates, and they will stop en route to demand more money, citing traffic or making up any excuse about protests causing problems. Instead, walk down the street towards MRT Sam Yan station or the other direction and insist on a metered taxi. It will always be less than half of the rate the tout-taxis will give you.
